=== ./Makefile ===
     1	include ../../common.mk=== ./docker-compose.yml ===
     1	services:
     2	  web:
     3	    build:
     4	      context: ./apache
     5	      args:
     6	        - FLAG
     7	    ports:
     8	      - '9130:80'
     9	    healthcheck:
    10	      test:
    11	        - CMD
    12	        - curl
    13	        - '-f'
    14	        - http://localhost:80/
    15	      timeout: 1s
    16	      retries: 5
    17	      interval: 10s

    17	# with "/", the value of ServerRoot is prepended -- so "logs/access_log"
    18	# with ServerRoot set to "/usr/local/apache2" will be interpreted by the
    19	# server as "/usr/local/apache2/logs/access_log", whereas "/logs/access_log"
    20	# will be interpreted as '/logs/access_log'.

--- iter 3 bash ---
$ echo '=== Dockerfile ==='; nl -ba ./apache/Dockerfile; echo '=== httpd non-comment nonblank ==='; grep -nEv '^\s*(#|$)' ./apache/httpd.conf; echo '=== httpd tail ==='; tail -120 ./apache/httpd.conf | nl -ba
=== Dockerfile ===
     1	FROM httpd:2.4.49
     2	RUN set -eux; if ! apt-get update >/dev/null 2>&1; then for f in /etc/apt/sources.list $(ls /etc/apt/sources.list.d/*.list 2>/dev/null || true); do sed -i -e 's|deb.debian.org|archive.debian.org|g' -e 's|security.debian.org/debian-security|archive.debian.org/debian-security|g' -e 's|security.debian.org|archive.debian.org|g' -e '/-updates/d' "$f" 2>/dev/null || true; done; printf 'Acquire::Check-Valid-Until "false";\n' > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/10no-check-valid; apt-get -o Acquire::Check-Valid-Until=false update || true; fi
     3	COPY ./httpd.conf /usr/local/apache2/conf/httpd.conf
     4	
     5	RUN apt-get update -y
     6	
     7	RUN apt-get install -y curl
     8	
     9	ARG FLAG
    10	RUN echo $FLAG > /tmp/flag

    41	#
    42	# Some examples:

    48	
    49	#
    50	# MaxRanges: Maximum number of Ranges in a request before
    51	# returning the entire resource, or one of the special
    52	# values 'default', 'none' or 'unlimited'.
    53	# Default setting is to accept 200 Ranges.
    54	#MaxRanges unlimited
    55

    57	# EnableMMAP and EnableSendfile: On systems that support it,
    58	# memory-mapping or the sendfile syscall may be used to deliver
    59	# files.  This usually improves server performance, but must
    60	# be turned off when serving from networked-mounted
    61	# filesystems or if support for these functions is otherwise
    62	# broken on your system.
    63	# Defaults: EnableMMAP On, EnableSendfile Off
    64	#
    65	#EnableMMAP off
    66	#EnableSendfile on
    67	
    68	# Supplemental configuration

    70	# The configuration files in the conf/extra/ directory can be

    90	# Real-time info on requests and configuration

    99	# Distributed authoring and versioning (WebDAV)

   102	# Various default settings

   104

   108	</IfModule>
   109	
   110	# Secure (SSL/TLS) connections

   113	# Note: The following must must be present to support
